---
title: "Cochran-Mantel-Haenszel (CMH) Test"
---

::: {.callout-tip}
## This chapter is a stub — contributions welcome
Fill it in following the two full exemplars
([Summary Statistics](summary-statistics.qmd),
[ANCOVA](ancova.qmd)) and the
[topic template](https://github.com/OWNER/submission-transparency/blob/main/templates/topic-template.qmd).
See the checklist in [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](../CONTRIBUTING.md).
:::

## What a reviewer wants

A test of association between treatment and a binary/ordinal response that
**adjusts for a stratification factor** (e.g. region), plus the common
Mantel-Haenszel odds-ratio estimate with its CI.

## Suggested minimal approach

- **Test:** base R `mantelhaen.test()` on a `treatment x response x stratum`
  array — no package needed. Use `correct = FALSE` to match SAS `FREQ` /CMH by
  default, and document the continuity-correction choice explicitly.
- **Estimate:** the M-H common odds ratio (base R gives it; `epitools` or a
  short hand-rolled M-H formula can add the CI).

## Simulation to build

- Simulate stratified 2x2 tables with a **known** common odds ratio and unequal
  stratum sizes / baseline risks.
- Confirm the CMH test holds its type-I error at the nominal level under the
  null, has the expected power under the known alternative, and that the M-H
  odds-ratio estimate is approximately unbiased on the log scale.
